ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic mushroom soup entry; Sup Cendawan is the Malay clear-broth variant. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Malay mushroom clear soup; A00GJ (Soups and broths) used as the closest defensible broader category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this specific Malay soup preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Sup Cendawan is approximately 300 ml (one bowl), providing about 75 kcal, 5.4 g protein, 1.2 g fat, and 10.5 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: IMR Malaysia MyFCD — Mushroom soup / clear vegetable broth with mushrooms; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entries for oyster mushroom (FDC ID 169403) and shiitake mushroom (FDC ID 168436)
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD; USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Sup Cendawan exists in IMR MyFCD. Values are adapted from IMR MyFCD clear soup / vegetable broth entries and USDA FoodData Central mushroom entries (oyster mushroom FDC 169403, shiitake FDC 168436). Sodium reflects typical seasoning with fish sauce or salt as described. Input AI-generated values were cross-checked against these approved sources and retained where consistent. vitamin_b12 is 0.0 as mushrooms and plant-based broth contain no B12; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mushrooms and Mushroom-Based Dishes — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for mushroom soup and low-starch vegetable broths
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Sup Cendawan. GI of 15 is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for mushroom-based soups and clear vegetable broths, which consistently show GI values in the 10–20 range. Mushrooms are low in starch and high in beta-glucan fibre, supporting a very low GI. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (15 × 10.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.6, rounded to 0.5 per 100 g basis.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 180 mg per 100 g; 540 mg per standard 300 ml serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size as one serving contributes approximately 540 mg sodium (about 23% of the 2300 mg daily limit); request reduced fish sauce or salt when preparing at home.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (estimated GI 15) and low available carbohydrate (3.5 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sup Cendawan is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low GI and minimal carbohydrate load; beta-glucan fibre from mushrooms may additionally support postprandial glucose control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.4 g per 100 g), negligible saturated fat, and no cholesterol from plant-based mushroom broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and cholesterol-free profile of Sup Cendawan makes it a heart-friendly choice; potassium (150 mg per 100 g) from mushrooms supports vascular health, though sodium should be moderated in those with concurrent hypertension.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sup Cendawan is a traditional Malay clear mushroom soup originating from Malaysia and widely consumed across Southeast Asia. The dish is prepared by simmering a variety of fresh mushrooms — commonly oyster mushrooms (cendawan tiram), shiitake, or wood ear mushrooms — in a light, aromatic broth seasoned with garlic, shallots, ginger, and white pepper. Vegetables such as carrots and spring onions are often added. Unlike Western cream-based mushroom soups, Sup Cendawan is a clear, low-calorie broth with a delicate umami flavour and a light, clean texture. It is typically served as a side dish or starter at lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, it is low in energy (approximately 25 kcal per 100 g), low in fat (0.4 g), and provides modest protein (1.8 g) and dietary fibre (1.0 g) from the mushrooms. Mushrooms contribute riboflavin (0.15 mg), niacin (1.8 mg), potassium (150 mg), and selenium (4.5 mcg per 100 g). The soup has a very low estimated glycemic index of approximately 15, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or seeking low-GI foods. Sodium content (180 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for those on sodium-restricted diets. The dish is naturally halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free; it becomes vegan when fish sauce is omitted and salt is used instead.
common_user_queries: Is Sup Cendawan good for diabetics?
How many calories in Malay mushroom soup?
What mushrooms are used in Sup Cendawan?
What is the glycemic index of mushroom soup?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Sup Cendawan exists in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from approved sources (IMR MyFCD clear soup entries and USDA FoodData Central mushroom entries) and cross-checked against the AI-generated input values, which were found to be broadly consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this Malay clear mushroom soup preparation is not available in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific preparation is not reported in the consulted approved sources. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): mushrooms and plant-based broth contain no vitamin B12; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central oyster mushroom (FDC 169403) and shiitake mushroom (FDC 168436) entries. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as the dish is plant-based (fish sauce, if used, contributes negligible cholesterol at this dilution). Dietary flags note: is_vegetarian and is_vegan are set to false by default as the traditional recipe may include fish sauce; the dish becomes vegetarian/vegan when fish sauce is replaced with salt. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 15 × 10.5 g available carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 1.6 per serving; gi_source field reports per-100g basis value of 0.5.
